label: Engineering Works and Planned Disruption Policy – All Networks

advance_notice.description: Passengers will be notified of planned engineering works at least 7 days in advance where possible.

short_notice_works.description: In exceptional circumstances (e.g. emergency safety works), engineering works may be announced with less than 7 days' notice.
short_notice_works.passenger_support: Customer service will be available to assist affected passengers with rebooking or refunds.

affected_bookings.notification: Passengers with confirmed bookings on affected services will be contacted directly via email and in-app notification.

options.description: Passengers may transfer their booking to an alternative scheduled service on the same route at no charge, subject to availability.
options.how_to: Contact customer service or rebook via the app using the disruption rebooking flow.

options.description: Passengers may cancel their affected booking and receive a full refund with no admin fee, regardless of when the cancellation is made.
Where available, replacement bus or rail services will be operated between affected stations. Details will be communicated with the disruption notice.

bus_replacement.description: Replacement bus services will operate between affected stations where rail services are suspended.
bus_replacement.ticket_validity: Existing rail tickets are valid on replacement bus services for the duration of the disruption. No additional fare is required.
bus_replacement.journey_time: Replacement bus journeys typically take longer than the equivalent rail journey. Passengers should allow extra time.
bus_replacement.accessibility: Accessible replacement buses will be provided where operationally possible. Passengers with accessibility requirements should contact customer service in advance.

partial_disruption.description: Where only part of a route is affected, passengers may be required to change at the boundary station and board a replacement service for the disrupted section.

refund_during_disruption.eligibility: Any passenger holding a valid ticket for a service that is cancelled or significantly altered due to engineering works or planned disruption is entitled to a full refund.
refund_during_disruption.how_to_claim: Submit a refund request via the app or customer service, quoting the booking reference and the affected service.

delay_compensation.description: If a replacement service results in a delay of 30 minutes or more compared to the original scheduled arrival time, passengers may be eligible for delay compensation under policy RF005.
delay_compensation.reference: See refund_policy.json → RF005 for compensation rules and how to claim.

passenger_responsibilities.description: Passengers are encouraged to check for disruption notices before travelling, particularly during holiday periods and weekends when engineering works are most commonly scheduled.
passenger_responsibilities.how_to_check: Check the operator app, website, or station display boards for the latest service information.
